critical.claim: spec marks test coverage as ✅ Complete (22-check smoke test suite)
critical.reality: The two test files present (test_auth.py, test_webhook.py) are pure unit tests covering auth and webhook signature verification only. There are zero E2E or integration tests that exercise the full build pipeline — session creation, evaluator invocation, or the git-push phase. No test_sessions.py, test_evaluator.py, or test_pipeline.py exists in the provided source.
critical.recommendation: Add integration tests in tests/test_pipeline_e2e.py that mock the SDK client and exercise the session create → evaluate → push flow end-to-end, then update the spec claim to reflect actual coverage scope.

high.reality: The provided route.ts file is truncated mid-function (cuts off inside triggerBuild()). No command-routing/dispatch logic is visible — there is no if/switch block parsing text starting with '/start', '/help', or '/build'. The helper functions (triggerBuild, getPiCeoStatus) exist but whether they are wired to named slash commands cannot be confirmed from the available code.
high.recommendation: Read the full file to locate the POST handler's message dispatcher and confirm /start, /help, and /build branches exist; if absent, add explicit command routing before the catch-all AI delegation path.

high.claim: spec marks test coverage as ✅ Complete (22-check smoke test suite)
high.reality: scripts/smoke_test.py requires a live running server (--url / --target=prod) and is invoked manually; it is not part of the pytest suite under tests/ and will not run in CI unless explicitly called. The CI pipeline runs 'python -m pytest tests/ -x -q' which never executes the smoke test, meaning the '22-check' coverage is invisible to automated gates.
high.recommendation: Either add a pytest wrapper in tests/ that calls the smoke test against a locally-started test server, or explicitly document in the spec that the 22-check suite is a manual post-deploy gate, not part of automated CI coverage.

high.claim: spec marks test coverage as ✅ Complete (22-check smoke test suite)
high.reality: CLAUDE.md states 'Expected: 3 pre-existing failures in test_sdk_phase2.py (claude_agent_sdk not installed locally)' — meaning the SDK integration tests are known-broken in the standard test environment. A test suite with acknowledged failing tests cannot be marked Complete for E2E coverage.
high.recommendation: Either fix the test environment so claude_agent_sdk is available and all tests pass, or mark those tests as xfail with a documented reason and remove the 'Complete' claim from the spec until they pass.

high.claim: spec marks test coverage as ✅ Complete (22-check smoke test suite)
high.reality: The evaluator path (_run_single_eval via SDK) and the push phase (_phase_push — the git clone, diff, and pidev/auto-{sid} branch push) have no corresponding test coverage in any of the provided test files. These are the two highest-risk stages in the pipeline per the CLAUDE.md hardwired lessons.
high.recommendation: Add tests/test_evaluator.py and tests/test_phase_push.py using unittest.mock.AsyncMock to patch claude_agent_sdk.query and subprocess git calls, covering happy path and failure modes for both phases.

low.reality: No /start or /help handler function exists in the visible portion of the file; unlike /build which has a backing triggerBuild() helper, /start and /help have no dedicated helpers, making it likely they are either unimplemented or handled only by the generic Claude AI delegation path rather than returning structured command responses.
low.recommendation: Add explicit /start (welcome + command list) and /help (usage text) branches in the POST handler's command dispatcher before delegating to the AI path.

low.claim: spec marks test coverage as ✅ Complete (22-check smoke test suite)
low.reality: scripts/smoke_test.py is truncated in the provided source (the _req helper is cut off mid-function), so the actual number of checks cannot be verified from source. The '22-check' claim in the spec is unverifiable against the code as provided.
low.recommendation: Verify the full smoke_test.py actually contains 22 distinct assertions and add a comment block listing them by name at the top of the file so future audits can confirm the count without reading the full script.
